summary: self_compress.py implements SelfCompressingPostFilter (3-layer dilated CNN with learnable per-channel bit-depth via STE+Lagrangian rate). It is a TRAINER for a separate postfilter module, not a generic compressor for arbitrary renderer.bin files. The dilated-h64 renderer.bin is already FP4-quantized (custom ASYM-magic format, 41 weight blobs, version=2 header, base_ch=36 mid_ch=60 motion_hidden=32 depth=1).

investigation.what_it_compresses: A specific 3-layer hidden=64 kernel=3 dilated postfilter CNN, NOT arbitrary models
investigation.renderer_bin_format: ASYM magic + 5,626 B JSON header + 41 per-layer FP4-quantized weight blobs

headroom_test_general_compressors_on_ren.note: Wrapping the FP4-packed renderer.bin in brotli q=11 saves ~12% (35 KB), worth ~0.023 rate score. This requires inflate-time decompression — confirm rule-compliant before adopting.

recommendation: NOT IMPLEMENTED YET — for the literal request (apply self-compression to renderer.bin). The self_compress.py module would have to be re-applied during a fresh renderer training run targeting this architecture. The fastest applicable win is wrapping renderer.bin in brotli (~12% saving, 0.023 rate), but the parent should verify inflate-side cost before deploy.
